questions.q: Who lived in North America for thousands of years before European settlement?
questions.explanation: Hundreds of Indigenous nations lived across the continent long before Europeans arrived.

questions.q: Which of these was a reason European powers came to North America?
options: Land, trade, wealth, and religious freedom
To join an existing United States
questions.explanation: Europeans sought land, trade, wealth, religious freedom, and national advantage.

questions.q: Which English settlement was founded in Virginia in 1607?
questions.explanation: Jamestown (1607) was England's first lasting colony; the Pilgrims reached Plymouth in 1620.

questions.q: What political tradition did the Atlantic colonies develop that influenced later American democracy?
options: Rule by a single governor for life
Self-government through local assemblies and town meetings
questions.explanation: Distance from London led colonists to govern themselves through elected assemblies.

questions.q: The Haudenosaunee (Iroquois) Confederacy is an example of what?
options: Several Indigenous nations united under a shared council
questions.explanation: It joined several nations under a common council — a sophisticated Indigenous government.
